memoryArtifacts.headline: Statue of Mastino II della Scala. Once located on the top of his tomb, one of the Scaliger Tombs, it is now in the Castelvecchio Museum at Verona.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Mastino II Della Scala於1308年出生時，他的父親Alboino della Scala , signore di Verona24歲，母親Beatrice Corregio22歲。他和Taddea da Carrara至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1351年6月3日在義大利維圖維洛納維洛納享年43歲，埋葬在義大利維圖維洛納維洛納。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mastino II Della Scala于1308年出生时，他的父亲Alboino della Scala , signore di Verona24岁，母亲Beatrice Corregio22岁。他和Taddea da Carrara至少有3个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1351年6月3日在意大利維圖維洛納維洛納享年43岁，埋葬在意大利維圖維洛納維洛納。

nameMeanings.name: Della
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Carmine, Geno, Amelio, Aniello, Concetta, Cosmo, Flavio, Gaetano, Gennaro, Mafalda, Raffaele.
Italian: habitational name from any of numerous minor places called Cella or Celle (from cella ‘cell’), because they were once the locations of hermits’ cells.

nameMeanings.name: Scala
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Luigi, Sal, Antonio, Carmine, Corrado, Orazio, Tiziana, Aldo, Angelo, Carlo, Carmela, Concetta.

Italian: topographic name from scala ‘ladder, flight of steps; wharf’ for someone who lived by a flight of steps or on terraced land; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Scala in Salerno province, Scala Coeli in Cosenza, or the district so named in Torregrotta, in Messina province, Sicily. Compare Di Scala and La Scala .
Americanized form of Czech Skála, Polish and Jewish (from Poland) Skała, or Slovak and Rusyn Skala .

variants.name: Di Scala
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Emidio, Luigi, Nicola, Paolo.
Italian: habitational name for someone from any of various places places named with scala ‘ladder, flight of steps; wharf’ (see Scala ).

variants.name: Scalese
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Gennaro, Guido, Pasquale, Vincenzo.
Italian (southern): probably a habitational name for someone from Scala in Salerno province (see   Scala  ).

variants.name: Scalera
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Vito, Angelo, Carmine, Ciro, Gilda, Lorenzo, Luciano, Pasquale.
Italian:
habitational name from a district so named in Filiano in Potenza province.

variants.name: La Scala
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Faust, Natale, Nicola, Silvio.
Italian (also Lascala): variant of Scala , with the addition of the definite article la.

variants.name: Skala
meanings: Czech (Skála); Slovak (also Skála); Polish and Jewish (from Poland) (Skała); Rusyn (from Slovakia and Poland; in Poland spelled Skała): topographic name for someone who lived by a rock or crag, Czech skála, Slovak and Rusyn skala, Polish skała. As a Jewish surname this is often artificial. Compare Scala and Skalla .

variants.name: Scali
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Domenic, Antonio, Geraldo, Orlando, Pasquale, Rocco, Carmela, Cesare, Fortunato, Giuseppe, Mauro, Pasqualina, Piero, Rosario, Saverio.
Italian (southern):
habitational name from Scali in Piedimonte Etneo, Sicily, named with Greek skali(n) ‘step, terrace’ (itself derived from Latin scala ‘ladder’), or from some other similarly named place.

nameMeanings.name: Mastino
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Martinus. See Martin .

variants.name: Martin
meanings: form of the Latin name Martinus. This was probably originally derived from Mars (genitive Martis), the name of the Roman god of war (and earlier of fertility). Martin became very popular in the Middle Ages, especially on the Continent, as a result of the fame of St Martin of Tours . He was born the son of a Roman officer in Upper Pannonia (an outpost of the Roman Empire, now part of Hungary), and, although he became a leading figure in the 4th-century Church, he is chiefly remembered now for having divided his cloak in two and given half to a beggar. The name was also borne by five popes, including one who defended Roman Catholic dogma against Eastern Orthodox theology. He died after suffering imprisonment and privations in Naxos and public humiliation in Constantinople, and was promptly acclaimed a martyr by supporters of the Roman Church. Among Protestants, the name is sometimes bestowed in honour of the German theologian Martin Luther ( 1483–1546 ); Martin was used as a symbolic name for the Protestant Church in satires by both Dryden and Swift. A further influence may be its use as the given name of the civil-rights leader Martin Luther King ( 1929–68 ).

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Wondrous’ or ‘enlightenment’.
2  ♀ ‘Fine jade’.

en.lifeSummary: When Mastino II Della Scala was born in 1308, his father, Alboino della Scala , signore di Verona, was 24 and his mother, Beatrice Corregio, was 22. He had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Taddea da Carrara. He died on 3 June 1351, in Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italy, at the age of 43, and was buried in Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italy.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mastino II Della Scala est né en 1308, son père, Alboino della Scala , signore di Verona, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Beatrice Corregio, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Taddea da Carrara. Il est décédé le 3 juin 1351, à Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italie, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italie.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mastino II Della Scala 1308 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alboino della Scala , signore di Verona 24 und seine Mutter Beatrice Corregio 22. Er hatte mit Taddea da Carrara mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 3. Juni 1351 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italien und wurde in Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mastino II Della Scala è nato il nel 1308, il suo padre, Alboino della Scala , signore di Verona, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Beatrice Corregio, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Taddea da Carrara. Morì il 3 giugno 1351, nel Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italia, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italia.

ja.lifeSummary: Mastino II Della Scalaが1308年に生まれた時，父Alboino della Scala , signore di Veronaは24歳，母Beatrice Corregioは22歳であった。彼はTaddea da Carraraとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1351年6月3日にイタリアベンエトーバーオナバーオナで43歳で死去し，イタリアベンエトーバーオナバーオナにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mastino II Della Scala(이)가 1308년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alboino della Scala , signore di Verona(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrice Corregio(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Taddea da Carrara(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1351년 6월 3일 이탈리아 븐토 베로나 베로나에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 이탈리아 븐토 베로나 베로나에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mastino II Della Scala nasceu em 1308, seu pai, Alboino della Scala , signore di Verona, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Beatrice Corregio, tinha 22 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas com Taddea da Carrara. Ele faleceu em 3 de junho de 1351, em Verona, Verona, Veneto, Itália, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Verona, Verona, Veneto, Itália.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mastino II Della Scala родился в 1308 г., его отцу по имени Alboino della Scala , signore di Verona было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Beatrice Corregio, 22. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Taddea da Carrara. Он умер 3 июня 1351 г. в местности вирона, вирона, винето, Италия в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности вирона, вирона, винето, Италия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mastino II Della Scala nació en 1308, su papá, Alboino della Scala , signore di Verona, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Beatrice Corregio, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas con Taddea da Carrara. Murió el 3 de junio de 1351, en Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italia, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italia.
